name: Summary demo: 22-turn sales call
description: A 22-turn discovery -> pricing -> close call (YOU = the rep/user, SPEAKER = the customer). Long enough that the every-10-turns Summarizer fires at turns 10 and 20. Watch the `summary` variable appear and update in the Blackboard tab, and open 'view exact prompt' on the reader agents to see the summary inside their prompts.
user_context: You are Alex, an account executive on a live discovery call. The copilot whispers private coaching to you.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: Thanks for making the time. I'd love to understand how your team handles this today before I show anything.
steps.note: Turn 1. Summarizer is gated off until turn 10 (mod condition) -> it shows under 'skipped: conditions_not_met'.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Sure. Honestly it's mostly spreadsheets right now, passed around over email.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: Got it. Where does that tend to break down for you?

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Things slip. Versions get out of sync, and someone always misses an update.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: How much time would you say that costs the team in a given week?

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Hard to say exactly... probably ten hours a week chasing things down.
steps.note: Key fact (10 hrs/week) the Summarizer will capture.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: And who's involved in that process day to day?

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Mostly the ops team, about five of us.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: That lines up with what I hear a lot. Before I show the platform -- what would 'better' look like for you?

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: One source of truth, fewer manual handoffs, and less time wasted.
steps.note: TURN 10 -> turn_count % 10 == 0, so the SUMMARIZER FIRES. Open the Blackboard tab: the `summary` variable appears.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: Perfect. Let me walk you through how teams like yours set that up.
steps.note: TURN 11 -> the summary is now visible to OTHER agents. Open 'view exact prompt' on Context-Aware Coach: the summary is rendered into its prompt.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Okay. How does it actually work in practice?

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: Everything lives in one workspace with automatic versioning, so nothing gets out of sync.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: That sounds useful. What does it cost?

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: It's tiered by seats -- let me show you the plan that fits a five-person team.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Hmm, that's more than we pay now for the spreadsheets.
steps.note: Cost objection. Context-Aware Coach fires, referencing the SUMMARY (the 10 hrs/week pain) to reframe on ROI.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: Fair -- though the spreadsheets are costing you ten hours a week. Let's look at it as time recovered.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: True. We've got around forty thousand a year budgeted for tooling, if it's the right fit.
steps.note: Budget signal (~$40k/yr) -- captured in the turn-20 summary.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: That works well. Let me make sure the fit is right for your workflow.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: Makes sense. So what would the next steps look like?
steps.note: TURN 20 -> SUMMARIZER FIRES AGAIN and the `summary` variable UPDATES (now includes pricing, objection, budget, buying signal). Deal Advisor fires too.

steps.speaker: YOU
steps.text: I'd suggest a two-week pilot with your ops team, then we review the time saved together.

steps.speaker: SPEAKER
steps.text: I like that. Send over a proposal and we'll get it moving.
steps.note: Close. Final summary persists in the Blackboard.
